summary: Plan 12 is execution-ready as a seven-task AWS integration closeout. Tasks 1 through 4 freeze and qualify one candidate, Task 5 deploys and exercises that candidate in two isolated AWS scenarios, Task 6 tears down every disposable surface, and Task 7 performs the exact-SHA release transition and ends the program. Hosted CI, Wardline, per-checkbox proof rows, post-plan checkpoints, and parent milestone closeout are outside this plan.

review_basis.process: The previously approved review was rebound after a focused material-delta review of the lean closeout and fresh-account cleanup corrections.

warnings.detail: Live execution still requires the approved AWS credentials, account and region, ECR repository, two bound Terraform scenarios, operator approvals, test identity, and cleanup authority. Their absence is a real Task 5 blocker, not a reason to add local proof work.

resolved_findings.resolution: Corrected cleanup-evidence-finalize calls to the controller's --file and --ledger interface.
Restored the minimum runnable ECR, saved-plan Terraform, scenario binding, destroy, cleanup checkpoint, and finalizer path in the canonical deployment runbook.
Moved retained CloudWatch and X-Ray completeness enforcement before the live phase can pass.

Removed the unrelated repository-wide CI policy sweep and duplicate coverage run; retained Ruff, formatting, strict typing, PostgreSQL integration, the complete suite, image validation, and live AWS acceptance.

verification: Both independent reviewers approved the repaired lean plan with no remaining execution blocker.
Plan 12 and deployment-runbook Bash fences pass bash -n.
250 focused acceptance-controller and deployment-runbook tests pass.
Ruff check, Ruff format check, strict mypy for the controller, and git diff --check pass.
Plan 12 is 465 lines, down from the prior 3244-line form, while real deployment and teardown commands remain in the reusable runbook.

review_history.summary: The full-plan review removed process-only choreography, repaired the concrete live execution gaps, and excluded unrelated repository-wide policy work from the AWS integration endpoint.
